hardPrerequisiteProof.label: Phase 1 journey contract and event freeze
Safety rulebook and outcome copy freeze

prerequisiteFreezeChecks.summary: request-type taxonomy remains Symptoms, Meds, Admin, Results
attachment policy retains quarantine-first upload + intake.attachment.quarantined
urgent_diversion_required and urgent_diverted remain distinct safety/result states

mergeGates.label: Backend contract merge gate
mergeGates.summary: Protects draft, submit, validation, attachment, safety, and promotion semantics from per-track drift.
verificationRules: No new public schema names outside the frozen Phase 1 seam set.
No duplicate event names for request.submitted or urgent-diversion transitions.
Required-field meaning remains bound to seq_140 question definitions.

mergeGates.label: Runtime/publication merge gate
mergeGates.summary: Keeps simulator-first storage, notification, outcome publication, and runtime tuple publication aligned.
verificationRules: Simulator-backed runtime tuples remain the only allowed baseline in Mock_now_execution.
Outcome/receipt/status publication uses frozen seq_139/142 artifacts rather than local UI inference.
Later live-provider bindings must supersede this gate pack instead of bypassing it.

mergeGates.label: Patient-shell integration merge gate
mergeGates.summary: Prevents backend/frontend divergence on same-shell continuity, route-state adapters, and outcome surface routing.
verificationRules: Patient shell route/state adapters stay bound to the single seq_139 journey contract.
Urgent_diversion_required and urgent_diverted stay distinct across backend and shell surfaces.
Save-state meaning, stale recovery, and resume blocking remain identical across backend and frontend tracks.

mergeGates.label: Test and accessibility merge gate
mergeGates.summary: Requires end-to-end proof, keyboard continuity, and diagram/table parity across the implementation block.
verificationRules: Each track must publish objective machine proof or browser proof aligned to its seam owners.
The patient shell must remain keyboard-traversable with reduced-motion parity.
No surface may invent alternate copy or labels that contradict the frozen ruleset.

prohibitions: No new public schema names outside the seq_139-seq_143 frozen Phase 1 seam set.
No duplicate event names for request.submitted, safety.urgent_diversion.required, or safety.urgent_diversion.completed.
No per-track redefinition of required-field meaning, request-type meaning, attachment scan state meaning, or save-state meaning.
No backend/frontend divergence on same-shell outcome routing, stale recovery, or promoted-draft supersession posture.

Mock_now_execution.summary: Open the parallel block against the browser-only, simulator-backed Phase 1 baseline.
allowed: Simulator-backed storage, notification, and runtime tuples.
Same-shell routing, receipt, and minimal status projections based on frozen seq_139-seq_142 semantics.
forbidden: Live-provider dependencies as a prerequisite for track completion.
Real auth or embedded work that changes route, draft, submit, or outcome semantics.
Unbounded DTO or event invention outside the seam list published here.

Actual_production_strategy_later.summary: Later live-provider or channel work must supersede this gate pack rather than bypassing it.
allowed: Reuse the same seam ownership, merge gates, and dependency graph where semantics are unchanged.
Publish explicit superseding gate packs if eligibility or merge ownership changes.
forbidden: Forking canonical submit, urgent-diversion, attachment quarantine, or receipt semantics.

parallelInterfaceGaps.summary: Authenticated uplift and embedded route-state adapters remain explicitly deferred. They may narrow chrome later, but they may not fork the draft, submit, or outcome semantics in this block.
Real downstream provider routing, live ETA accuracy, and non-simulator notification transport remain later superseding work. Current tracks must publish simulator-first runtime truth only.
Later sign-in uplift may add identity linkage and protected status retrieval, but it must reuse the same draft/public IDs, supersession rules, and resume postures frozen here.

tracks.trackLabel: Draft session lease and autosave API
tracks.capabilityOwned: Own the draft session lease boundary, autosave patch API, and resume-token-safe draft mutation cadence.

producedDownstreamArtifacts.intent: stable patch/write contract for UI and resume

tracks.liveProviderLaterNote: Real auth or embedded routing may reuse the lease boundary later, but may not change the draft/public ID semantics.

tracks.trackLabel: Submission envelope validation and required-field rules
tracks.capabilityOwned: Own pre-submit readiness evaluation, required-field truth, and machine-readable validation output aligned to the frozen questionnaire contract.

producedDownstreamArtifacts.intent: single meaning shared by par_156 and par_161

tracks.liveProviderLaterNote: Later auth or provider work may consume the same readiness verdicts but may not redefine required-field meaning.

tracks.trackLabel: Attachment upload, scan, quarantine, and document reference pipeline
tracks.capabilityOwned: Own signed upload initiation, quarantine-first scan settlement, duplicate replay, and draft-safe document reference projection.

producedDownstreamArtifacts.intent: quarantine/clean outcome for UI and promotion

tracks.liveProviderLaterNote: Live scanning or storage may replace simulator internals later, but the quarantine-first contract stays unchanged.

tracks.trackLabel: Contact preference capture and masked storage
tracks.capabilityOwned: Own contact preference persistence, masked projection output, and confirmation-safe storage semantics.
tracks.liveProviderLaterNote: Real channel providers may change delivery transport later, but not the stored contact-preference grammar.

tracks.trackLabel: Submission snapshot freeze and promotion transaction
tracks.capabilityOwned: Own the exact promotion transaction from draft snapshot to canonical request, including settlement chain and immutable freeze record.
tracks.liveProviderLaterNote: Later downstream systems must consume the same promotion record rather than replacing the submit boundary.

tracks.trackLabel: Free-text normalization into canonical request shape
tracks.capabilityOwned: Own deterministic normalization from questionnaire/draft text into canonical request fields without changing request-type meaning.
tracks.liveProviderLaterNote: Normalization may grow later, but it must continue to emit the same canonical request shape and event meaning.

tracks.trackLabel: Rule-based synchronous safety engine
tracks.capabilityOwned: Own synchronous safety screening, rule evaluation, and explicit urgent_diversion_required versus urgent_diverted state separation.
tracks.liveProviderLaterNote: Later clinical calibration may supersede thresholds, but not the canonical outcome state split or event names.

tracks.trackLabel: Urgent-diversion settlement and receipt grammar
tracks.capabilityOwned: Own settlement records and receipt grammar selection after safety screening, including hard-stop urgent handoff truth.
tracks.liveProviderLaterNote: Later handoff channels may change transport, but not the issued-versus-required urgent state distinction.

tracks.trackLabel: Triage task creation, ETA engine, and minimal status tracking
tracks.capabilityOwned: Own simulator-first triage task projection, ETA calculation, and minimal track-my-request status spine.
tracks.liveProviderLaterNote: Live ETA and provider orchestration may refine estimates later, but the minimal status spine remains the same seam.

tracks.trackLabel: Confirmation notification dispatch and observability
tracks.capabilityOwned: Own queued confirmation payload construction, channel-safe wording, and observability for dispatch attempts.
tracks.liveProviderLaterNote: Real delivery channels may replace simulator transport later, but not the payload grammar or observability envelope.

tracks.trackLabel: Promoted-draft token supersession and resume blocking
tracks.capabilityOwned: Own post-promotion draft invalidation, stale resume blocking, and replacement-token acknowledgement semantics.
tracks.liveProviderLaterNote: Later authenticated uplift must reuse this supersession contract rather than inventing alternate stale-token meanings.

tracks.trackLabel: Patient intake mission frame
tracks.capabilityOwned: Own the same-shell mission frame, route shell, persistent chrome, and shared state adapters for all Phase 1 intake child views.

producedDownstreamArtifacts.intent: route family foundation for par_156 through par_163
shared shell seam consumed by all frontend tracks

tracks.liveProviderLaterNote: Embedded and authenticated surfaces may later narrow the shell, but they must reuse this route/state adapter seam.

tracks.trackLabel: Request-type selection and progressive question flow
tracks.capabilityOwned: Own request-type selection, progressive question rendering, and review-safe supersession of hidden answers.
tracks.liveProviderLaterNote: Later provider or auth work may not alter request-type meaning or hidden-answer supersession behavior.

tracks.trackLabel: Quiet autosave status strip and resume states
tracks.capabilityOwned: Own quiet autosave feedback, save-state truth, and resume/stale banners inside the mission frame.
tracks.liveProviderLaterNote: Real auth refresh later may reuse the same stale/resume postures but may not rename or reinterpret them.

tracks.trackLabel: File upload evidence states and error recovery
tracks.capabilityOwned: Own file upload UX, quarantine-safe evidence states, duplicate replay messaging, and recovery actions.
tracks.liveProviderLaterNote: Later external storage/scan engines may not change quarantine-first UX truth or duplicate replay semantics.

tracks.trackLabel: Contact preference editor and confirmation copy
tracks.capabilityOwned: Own contact preference editing, masked confirmation copy, and confirmation-channel disclosure in the capture flow.
tracks.liveProviderLaterNote: Later real delivery channels may refine labels, but not the masked copy or preference grammar.

tracks.trackLabel: Same-shell urgent diversion surface
tracks.capabilityOwned: Own the urgent diversion surface inside the same shell, including required-versus-completed urgency truth and safe navigation handoff.
tracks.liveProviderLaterNote: Later embedded or live handoff transports may change the grant target, not the same-shell urgent truth model.

tracks.trackLabel: Same-shell receipt and ETA surface
tracks.capabilityOwned: Own same-shell receipt, ETA/status summary, and confirmation copy presentation after a non-urgent submit.
tracks.liveProviderLaterNote: Live ETA updates later must reuse the same receipt/ETA projection seam rather than reworking the shell route.

tracks.trackLabel: Minimal track-my-request page
tracks.capabilityOwned: Own the minimal track-my-request page using the simulator-first status/ETA projection and exact requestPublicId semantics.
tracks.liveProviderLaterNote: Authenticated track-my-request later may add identity checks, but it must reuse the same request/status projection seam.

tracks.trackLabel: Sign-in uplift, refresh, and resume postures
tracks.capabilityOwned: Own sign-in uplift messaging, refresh recovery, and resume postures without changing the simulator-first browser baseline.

producedDownstreamArtifacts.intent: final continuity surface for later auth supersession

tracks.liveProviderLaterNote: This track only publishes bounded placeholders for later auth/embedded work and must not make them current prerequisites.

assumptions.summary: Phase 1 completion in this block assumes simulator-backed runtime, storage, and notification seams only.
All tracks consume seq_139-seq_142 machine-readable contracts without silent local variants.

risks.summary: Receipt or urgent surfaces could drift from backend settlement truth if shell tracks bypass the shared seams.
risks.mitigation: Bind shell tracks to MG_143_PATIENT_SHELL_INTEGRATION and the shared route/state seam.

risks.summary: Simulator-first triage, ETA, or notification details could leak into later live-provider semantics.
risks.mitigation: Keep later provider/auth work explicitly deferred as PARALLEL_INTERFACE_GAP rows and require superseding gate packs.

conflicts.summary: Tracks may not merge new public DTO or event names unless they are already reserved by the seam list published here.

sourcePrecedence: blueprint/phase-1-the-red-flag-gate.md#1A. Journey contract and intake schema lock
blueprint/phase-1-the-red-flag-gate.md#1E. Submit normalization and synchronous safety engine
